# 6.3 Ranger Skill

For **set** , you can set traps up to a difficulty equal to your thief skill level without failing (you cannot set traps of a higher level). If a third party attempts to _sense_ , _find_ , or _remove_ a trap set by a player, the game master must attempt a success roll on their behalf. 

## **Ranger Skill** 

## **6.3** 

## **[** ② **First-Aid [-] ]** 

Owners of the ranger skill demonstrate a variety of abilities in outdoor activities, as explained below. 

## **(No equipment restriction)** 

If an adventurer's life force falls to 0 or less due to wounds, it will not recover naturally and there is a risk of death if left untreated. **First-aid** provided by a ranger will restore your life force to 1 point, allowing you to regain consciousness. It cannot fail, but it will take about 30 minutes of game time, and it cannot be done while in a state where you can't focus, such as in the middle of combat, etc. 

The ranger skill is also limited by equipment, just like the thief skill, if you want to make full use of its abilities. The armor you wear must have a required strength of up to _half_ your own strength, and must be ring mail or non-metal 

The ranger skill has some similar abilities to the thief skill. However, while the thief skill can be used both outdoors and indoors, the ranger skill is mostly useful only outdoors. 

## **[** ③ **Camouflage** 

① Footprint ⑨ Map Creation Tracking ② First Aid ⑩ Weather Prediction ③ Camouflage ⑪ Plant and Animal Check ④ Listen ⑫ Projectile Use ⑤ Sense Danger ⑬ Follow ⑥ Stealthy Step ⑭ Surprise Attack ⑦ Survivalism ⑮ Find/Sense Trap ⑧ Search ⑯ Remove/Set Trap 

## **[Intelligence] ]** 

**(Equipment restriction)** 

This is the ability to hide behind rocks, hide in bushes, or use such things to hide something. Since it's an ability that uses natural terrain, it cannot be used indoors. 

Checks are made in the same way as the thief skill's Hide (see p. 114). 

## **[** ④ **Listen [Intelligence] ]** 

## **(Equipment restriction)** 

Rangers can listen to even the smallest sounds and can choose the sound they want to hear from a wide range of sounds. This is handed the same way as the thief's ability. 

## **[** ① **Footprint Tracking [Intelligence] ]** 

## **(No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

This is the ability to track footprints left on the ground. The game master determines the difficulty by taking into account factors such as the condition of the ground and the amount of time that has passed since the footprints were made. 

## **[** ⑤ **Sense Danger [Intelligence]]** 

## **(No equipment restriction)** 

Rangers acquire a good intuition, which can be called a sixth sense. This ability is the ability used to sense when danger is approaching. For example, when you walk through a ravine and see a pebble fall, you may feel like a landslide 

is about to occur, or you may feel an arrow flying from the darkness. The target score is determined by the game master depending on the situation, but in general it should be set high. 

## **[** ⑥ **Stealthy Step [Agility] ]** 

## **(Equipment restriction)** 

Rangers can walk silently and unnoticed. This is handled the same way as the thief skill's ability. 

## **[** ⑦ **Survivalism [Life Force] ]** 

## **(No equipment restriction)** 

Rangers have acquired the ability to endure difficult situations brought about by disaster, such as surviving with only a small amount of water or avoiding freezing to death in winter mountains, and either survive until help arrives or 

**==> picture [162 x 300] intentionally omitted <==**

116

escape from a predicament on their own. When an adventurer's survival is in danger, the game master should set a target score and have them make a success roll to overcome the situation. 

## **[** ⑧ **Search [Intelligence] ] (No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

This is the ability to search for hidden things. The game master should determine the difficulty depending on what’s hidden. Since it's an ability that relies on feeling out of place in nature, it can _only_ be used outdoors. 

## **[** ⑨ **Map Creation [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

Rangers have a good sense of direction and can map out the places they've been. You can also determine direction from the position of the sun or stars. When creating a map, the game master determines a difficulty, and a success roll must be made to determine whether or not the map was drawn correctly. 

## **[** ⑩ **Weather Prediction [Intelligence] ]** 

## **(No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

Rangers have the ability to predict the weather. Rangers can measure the humidity using their own senses, and they also use small knowledge such as _birds that eat small insects such as flies fly close to the ground = no updraft = thick clouds = rain_ . From these combinations, you can roughly predict the weather. If a 

ranger succeeds on a success roll against a set target score using _ranger skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score, the ranger can roughly predict the weather (sunny, cloudy, rain, snow, storm, strong wind...... etc.) The game master must decide whether to use a difficulty or a target score. Normally you would use a target score. This is because failure means that the weather is unpredictable, and this is also very clear to the characters as well. 

If you attempt to use this ability and fail the success roll, you simply cannot predict the weather; it does _not_ predict the opposite. 

## **[** ⑪ **Plant and Animal Check [Intelligence] ]** 

## **(No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

Rangers can determine what animals and plants they see. When an animal or plant appears as a monster, you can make a success roll using its _rarity_ as the target score, similar to the sage skill's _monster check_ ability (the sage skill's abilities are explained later on page 118). 

In other cases (such as searching for herbs), the _game master_ should determine a target score for the check. 

## **[** ⑫ **Projectile Use [Dexterity, Strength] ]** 

## **(No equipment restriction)** 

The ranger skill is treated the same as the fighter skill (attack power, bonus damage modifiers) _only_ when attacking with projectiles. You can use any projectile as long as your strength allows it. The ranger skill is _not_ relevant to the 

use of or defense from melee weapons. Adventurers attack with the ranger skill and defend with the fighter or thief skill. When using the thief skill for defense, you must be lightly armed (required strength of weapons and armor must be _half_ your own strength or less). 

## **[** ⑬ **Follow [Agility] (Equipment restriction)** 

This handled the same way as the thief skill's ability of the same name. However, it can _only_ be used in nature. It _cannot_ be used indoors or in a city. 

## **[** ⑭ **Surprise Attack [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction)** 

The ranger skill is used for surprise attacks. For details, see 10.3: Surprise Attacks (see p. 138). 

## **[** ⑮ **Find/Sense Trap [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

This is basically the same ability as that of the thief skill, but it _only_ applies to traps that use outdoor nature. This includes those that are made by tying grass into a ring, or are hung from a tree using string, etc. Indoor mechanical traps cannot be _found/sensed_ with the ranger skill. 

## **[** ⑯ **Remove/Set Trap [Dexterity]] (Equipment restriction, not retriable)** 

This also follows the thief skill. It's the ability to remove traps set outdoors and create traps using natural objects. Indoor mechanical traps cannot be _removed_ or _set_ .
